questions.text: Which Indian state became the first to implement the Uniform Civil Code (UCC) post-independence?
questions.explanation: Uttarakhand became the first state to implement the Uniform Civil Code in 2024.

questions.text: The 'PM Surya Ghar: Muft Bijli Yojana' aims to provide up to how many units of free electricity per month?
questions.explanation: The scheme targets 300 units per month to promote solar energy adoption.

questions.text: India's longest sea bridge, Atal Setu, connects Mumbai to Alibaug.
questions.explanation: Atal Setu connects Mumbai to Navi Mumbai, not Alibaug.

questions.text: ISRO's Aditya-L1 mission is designed to study:
questions.explanation: Aditya-L1 is India's solar mission to study the Sun's corona.

questions.text: Which of the following were part of the BRICS expansion in 2024? (Select all correct)
questions.explanation: Egypt, Ethiopia, and Saudi Arabia joined BRICS in the 2024 expansion.

questions.text: Assertion: COP28 was held in Dubai. Reason: Dubai is in a developing nation.
questions.explanation: The assertion is true, but the reason is false as UAE is developing.

questions.text: Which country won the ICC Men's Cricket World Cup 2023?
questions.explanation: Australia defeated India in the 2023 Cricket World Cup final.

questions.text: Match the following missions with their targets:
questions.explanation: These are India's major space missions targeting different celestial bodies.

questions.text: Who is the current Secretary-General of the United Nations?
questions.explanation: António Guterres is serving as UN Secretary-General since 2017.

questions.text: India's first semiconductor fab will be established by Tata Group and PSMC in ______.
questions.explanation: The fab is being set up in Gujarat as part of the semiconductor mission.

questions.text: What is the name of the military operation launched by the US against Iran in February 2026?
questions.explanation: The US launched 'Operation Epic Fury' involving massive strikes on Tehran in February 2026.

questions.text: The Druzhba Oil Pipeline primarily transports oil from Central Asia to China.
questions.explanation: The Druzhba Oil Pipeline transports oil from Russia to Europe.

questions.text: Which prestigious Israeli award was conferred upon PM Modi in February 2026?
options: B. Speaker of the Knesset Medal
questions.explanation: PM Modi was conferred with Israel's prestigious 'Speaker of the Knesset Medal'.

questions.text: A new species of midge discovered in India in February 2026 is named the ______ Blossom Midge.
questions.explanation: A new Jasmine Blossom Midge species was discovered in India, adding to the country's biodiversity.

questions.text: Assertion: India's new GDP calculation series will not use UPI transaction data. Reason: UPI transactions have been completely banned in India.
questions.explanation: The new GDP series will exclude UPI transaction data, but UPI transactions are certainly not banned in India.

questions.text: Where did the 7th edition of Exercise Dharma Guardian commence in February 2026?
questions.explanation: Exercise Dharma Guardian's 7th edition commenced in Chaubattia, Uttarakhand.

questions.text: Match the following government initiatives with their primary focus:
questions.explanation: These represent key government programs spanning security, culture, and women's empowerment.

questions.text: In February 2026, the Union Cabinet approved a bill to rename the state of Kerala to 'Keralam'.
questions.explanation: The Union Cabinet approved a bill in February 2026 to rename Kerala to 'Keralam'.

questions.text: For which country did the Indian Embassy issue a safety advisory in February 2026 due to regional tensions?
questions.explanation: The Indian Embassy issued an advisory for Iran due to ongoing regional tensions and military operations.

questions.text: The Election Commission of India introduced a unified voting plan in February 2026 called the 'One ______ List'.
questions.explanation: The 'One Voter List' is the ECI's unified voting plan.

questions.text: Which Indian film won Best Children's & Family Film at the BAFTA Awards in 2026?
questions.explanation: India's 'Boong' won the Best Children's & Family Film at the BAFTA Awards 2026.

questions.text: Which of the following are features of the Vanjeevi Didi Initiative? (Select all correct)
options: A. Empowering women in wildlife reserves
B. Based in Palamu Tiger Reserve
questions.explanation: The initiative focuses on empowering women in the Palamu Tiger Reserve, Jharkhand.

questions.text: Where will the first phase of the Door-to-Door Housing Census 2027 start?
questions.explanation: The first phase of the Door-to-Door Housing Census 2027 is slated to begin in Ludhiana.

questions.text: The Vibrant Villages Programme-II was launched in the state of Assam.
questions.explanation: It was launched in Assam's Cachar District in February 2026.

questions.text: Assertion: 'Pax Silica' is a new alliance redefining global power. Reason: It is primarily focused on agricultural trade agreements.
questions.explanation: Pax Silica is an alliance redefining global power through semiconductors, not agriculture.

questions.text: What major decision did the International Paralympic Committee (IPC) take regarding Russia in February 2026?
options: B. Lifted the Russian Paralympics ban
questions.explanation: The IPC lifted the Russian Paralympics ban for the 2026 Games.

questions.text: MeitY rolled out ______ for next-generation multilingual voice AI in February 2026.
questions.explanation: VoicERA was developed by MeitY as a next-gen multilingual voice AI tool.

questions.text: Which tech company announced a $15 billion investment for a full-stack AI hub in Visakhapatnam?
questions.explanation: Google announced a $15 billion investment for an AI hub in Visakhapatnam.

questions.text: Match the recent AI and tech initiatives with their descriptions:
questions.explanation: These are prominent technology and sustainability portals/tools introduced recently in India.

questions.text: NHAI launched the 'Bee Corridors' initiative to create the first-ever pollinator initiative along national highways.
questions.explanation: Bee Corridors is NHAI's first-ever pollinator initiative launched along highways.

questions.text: How many Rafale fighter jets does India plan to acquire in the new deal announced in February 2026?
questions.explanation: India plans to acquire 114 Rafale fighter jets in the deal with France.

questions.text: Which of the following initiatives were launched by Kiren Rijiju in February 2026? (Select all correct)
questions.explanation: He launched the NIGRANI App, AI Chatbots, and the Haj Wrist Band.

questions.text: Which Indian female cricketer bagged the BBC Top Honor in February 2026?
questions.explanation: Smriti Mandhana received the BBC Top Honor marking a golden year for women's cricket.

questions.text: AIIMS conducted its first remote ultrasound procedure in the icy region of ______ in February 2026.
questions.explanation: The successful remote ultrasound procedure in Antarctica showcased India's telemedicine capabilities.

questions.text: What is 'Bulbul V-3', created in collaboration with Sarvam AI?
options: C. An initiative redefining Indian digital public infrastructure
questions.explanation: Bulbul V-3 is an AI initiative redefining Indian digital public infrastructure at a global level.

questions.text: Assertion: Odisha is introducing QR codes on land records. Reason: QR codes will facilitate easy digital verification of the documents.
questions.explanation: Odisha announced this measure to streamline and secure land record verification.

questions.text: Where was the 14th WTO Ministerial Conference (MC14) held in March 2026?
questions.explanation: MC14 was held in Yaoundé, Cameroon, under the chairmanship of Luc Magloire Mbarga Atangana.

questions.text: The 28-year e-commerce moratorium on customs duties for digital transmissions was permanently extended at the WTO MC14.
questions.explanation: The moratorium officially lapsed, causing the 'Yaoundé Deadlock' and marking a turning point in digital trade.

questions.text: What is the primary purpose of the 'Jan Samarth Portal'?
questions.explanation: It is a national portal for Credit-Linked Government Schemes to connect citizens digitally with these programs.

questions.text: India's First Digital Census 2027 is scheduled to begin its operations in ______ 2026.
questions.explanation: The digital census is scheduled to begin in April 2026, featuring self-enumeration.

questions.text: Which country announced a ban on social media for users under 16 years old in March 2026?
questions.explanation: Indonesia banned social media for under-16 users, establishing strict new digital rules.

questions.text: Match the March 2026 discoveries/events with their locations:
questions.explanation: These events highlight significant geographical, biological, and geopolitical occurrences from March 2026.

questions.text: How many railway stations are targeted for redevelopment under the Amrit Bharat Station Scheme?
questions.explanation: The scheme aims to redevelop 1338 railway stations across India.

questions.text: Which of the following states passed the Uniform Civil Code (UCC) Bill in the first quarter of 2026? (Select all correct)
questions.explanation: Uttarakhand implemented it first, and Gujarat passed its UCC Bill in March 2026.

questions.text: Assertion: BCCI decided to skip the opening ceremony at Chinnaswamy Stadium for IPL 2026. Reason: The stadium was completely destroyed in an earthquake.
questions.explanation: The assertion is true, but the reason is fabricated. The ceremony was skipped for logistical/operational reasons, not an earthquake.

questions.text: According to reports from March 2026, which country topped the global pollution index?
questions.explanation: Pakistan topped the global pollution list, while India ranked sixth.

questions.text: The Lok Sabha passed the ______ Rights Amendment Bill in March 2026 to advance LGBTQ+ protections.
questions.explanation: The Transgender Rights Amendment Bill 2026 was passed to strengthen protections.

questions.text: What is 'Akasha300 3D', delivered to ISRO by Spacetime 4D?
questions.explanation: Akasha300 3D is a 3D printer delivered for space applications.

questions.text: The Ayush Ministry launched an in-flight yoga routine for air travellers in March 2026.
questions.explanation: This innovative initiative aims to promote wellness during air travel.

questions.text: Which hill was declared a National Geo-Heritage Site in March 2026?
questions.explanation: Kalinjar Hill was declared a National Geo-Heritage Site, recognizing its geological importance.

questions.text: Which Indian state launched the 'Green Corridor 2026' for electric vehicles?
questions.explanation: Maharashtra launched the Green Corridor 2026 to promote EV infrastructure on major highways.

questions.text: The 2026 G20 Summit is scheduled to be held in Brazil.
questions.explanation: Brazil holds the G20 presidency for the 2026 summit.

questions.text: In April 2026, the RBI announced a new ______ policy to curb inflation.
questions.explanation: The RBI frequently adjusts monetary policy to manage liquidity and inflation.

questions.text: Which of these Indian cities are part of the 'Smart City Mission'? (Select all correct)
questions.explanation: Pune, Surat, Indore, and Bhubaneshwar are prominent cities under the Smart City Mission.

questions.text: Assertion: Solar energy is becoming cheaper in India. Reason: Increased government subsidies and technological advancements have reduced costs.
questions.explanation: Policy support and efficiency gains are driving down solar energy costs in India.

questions.text: Who is the Chief Justice of India as of April 2026?
questions.explanation: D.Y. Chandrachud serves as the Chief Justice of India in 2026.

questions.text: The 'Nari Shakti Vandan Adhiniyam' provides reservation for women in the Lok Sabha.
questions.explanation: The Women's Reservation Bill ensures 33% reservation for women in the Lok Sabha.

questions.text: The Indian Navy's recent exercise in the Indian Ocean was named ______.
questions.explanation: Exercise MILAN is a major biennial naval exercise hosted by the Indian Navy.

questions.text: Which of these are major space agencies? (Select all correct)
questions.explanation: ISRO, NASA, ESA, and JAXA are national/regional space agencies. UNICEF is a child-focused UN organization.

questions.text: Match the government ministries to their ministers (as of April 2026):
questions.explanation: These are current ministerial portfolios in the Indian government.

questions.text: Which organization recently released the 'World Economic Outlook 2026' report?
questions.explanation: The IMF regularly publishes the World Economic Outlook report.

questions.text: The 'Gaganyaan' mission aims to send Indian astronauts into space.
questions.explanation: Gaganyaan is ISRO's human spaceflight program.

questions.text: The new headquarters of the RBI is located in ______.
questions.explanation: The central office of the Reserve Bank of India is in Mumbai.

questions.text: Which of these are sustainable energy types? (Select all correct)
questions.explanation: Wind, solar, and hydroelectric are sustainable/renewable sources.

questions.text: Assertion: Global temperatures are rising. Reason: Greenhouse gas emissions from human activities are the primary cause.
questions.explanation: Scientific consensus confirms that human-induced emissions are the primary driver of climate change.

questions.text: Who is the Prime Minister of the United Kingdom as of April 2026?
questions.explanation: Keir Starmer serves as the UK Prime Minister.

questions.text: The FIFA World Cup 2026 will be hosted by North America.
questions.explanation: USA, Canada, and Mexico are jointly hosting the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

questions.text: The ______ agreement focuses on global climate change targets.
questions.explanation: The Paris Agreement is an international treaty on climate change adopted in 2015.

questions.text: Which of these are Indian payment systems? (Select all correct)
questions.explanation: UPI, IMPS, and RTGS are key pillars of the Indian digital payment infrastructure.

questions.text: Match countries with their currencies:
questions.explanation: These are the official legal tender for the specified countries.

questions.text: The 'Vande Bharat' trains are a symbol of which sector in India?
questions.explanation: Vande Bharat trains represent the modernization of Indian Railways.

questions.text: The G7 consists of seven industrialized nations.
questions.explanation: The G7 includes Canada, France, Germany, Italy, Japan, UK, and the US.

questions.text: The ______ is the highest mountain in the world.
questions.explanation: Mount Everest is the highest peak above sea level.

questions.text: Which of these are major social media platforms? (Select all correct)
questions.explanation: Instagram, X, LinkedIn, and Reddit are prominent social media networks.

questions.text: Assertion: Inflation affects the purchasing power of money. Reason: Higher prices for goods and services mean one unit of currency buys less.
questions.explanation: Inflation inherently reduces the amount of goods that can be bought for a fixed amount of currency.

questions.text: Which state launched the 'Mission Shakti' for women's development?
questions.explanation: Odisha's Mission Shakti is a flagship program for women's self-help groups.

questions.text: India is a member of the G20.
questions.explanation: India is a key member of the G20 group of major economies.

questions.text: The ______ Bank was established in 1935 as the central bank of India.
questions.explanation: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) was established on April 1, 1935.

questions.text: Which of these cities are in India? (Select all correct)
questions.explanation: Delhi, Mumbai, Chennai, and Kolkata are major Indian cities.

questions.text: Match inventors to their inventions:
questions.explanation: These are world-changing historical inventions.

questions.text: Which planet is known as the 'Red Planet'?
questions.explanation: Mars appears red due to iron oxide on its surface.

questions.text: The Sun is a star.
questions.explanation: The Sun is a G-type main-sequence star at the center of our solar system.

questions.text: The chemical symbol for water is ______.
questions.explanation: H2O represents two hydrogen atoms and one oxygen atom.

questions.text: Which of these are continents? (Select all correct)
questions.explanation: Africa, Asia, Europe, and Australia are continents. India is a country.

questions.text: Assertion: Plants are green. Reason: They contain chlorophyll, which absorbs light for photosynthesis.
questions.explanation: Chlorophyll gives plants their green color and is essential for converting light into energy.

questions.text: Which is the largest country in the world by population?
questions.explanation: India has the world's largest population.

questions.text: The moon orbits the Earth.
questions.explanation: The Moon is Earth's only natural satellite, orbiting it once every 27.3 days.

questions.text: The ______ is the center of our solar system.
questions.explanation: Everything in our solar system orbits around the Sun.

questions.text: Which of these are noble gases? (Select all correct)
questions.explanation: Helium, neon, and argon are noble gases in Group 18 of the periodic table.

questions.text: Match the following:
questions.explanation: These are the three common states of water.

questions.text: Which ocean is located between the US and Europe?
questions.explanation: The Atlantic Ocean separates North America from Europe and Africa.

questions.text: A day on Earth is 24 hours.
questions.explanation: A day on Earth is defined by one full rotation on its axis, roughly 24 hours.

questions.text: The ______ is the largest ocean on Earth.
questions.explanation: The Pacific Ocean is the largest and deepest of the five oceans.

questions.text: Which of these are renewable energy sources? (Select all correct)
questions.explanation: Solar, wind, and hydro are renewable. Oil and coal are non-renewable.

questions.text: Assertion: Humans need oxygen to survive. Reason: Oxygen is vital for cellular respiration.
questions.explanation: Oxygen is a primary reactant in the process that generates energy in our cells.

questions.text: Who was the first person to step on the moon?
questions.explanation: Neil Armstrong was the first human to step on the Moon in 1969.

questions.text: The equator receives direct sunlight year-round.
questions.explanation: Because of Earth's orientation, the equator is the region that receives the most direct solar energy.

questions.text: The largest continent is ______.
questions.explanation: Asia is the largest continent by both area and population.

questions.text: Which of these are planets in our solar system? (Select all correct)
questions.explanation: Mars, Jupiter, Venus, and Saturn are planets. The Moon is a satellite.

questions.text: Water freezes at 0 degrees Celsius.
questions.explanation: At sea-level atmospheric pressure, water freezes at 0°C.
